heroHeadline: SCOTUS Weighs TPS for 350K Haitians; 2nd Circuit Strikes ICE Detention Policy — Trump Approval Hits Record 34%
heroSubtitle: On April 29, the Supreme Court heard oral arguments in Mullin v. Dahlia Doe and Trump v. Miot — consolidating challenges to Trump's termination of Temporary Protected Status for roughly 350,000 Haitians and 6,000 Syrians, with a ruling expected by late June. A day earlier, the Second Circuit unanimously struck down the administration's mandatory ICE detention policy, creating a circuit split that will likely reach the Supreme Court. A Reuters/Ipsos poll released April 28 found Trump's approval at 34% — the lowest of his second term — with just 22% approving his cost-of-living handling and 64% saying the Iran war is 'not worth the cost.' On the Iran war front (Day 61), Trump warned Tehran to 'get smart soon,' the UAE announced an OPEC exit compounding oil market disruption, and the ceasefire remains frozen with no talks scheduled.
footerNote: Data sourced from White House, Federal Register, executive orders, court records, Al Jazeera, CNN, Reuters, AP, NPR, Bloomberg, CNBC, Axios, NBC News, CBS News, Fox News, Gulf News, The National, Newsweek, Nate Silver/Silver Bulletin, YouGov/Economist, Fox News, BLS, Peterson Institute for International Economics, Tax Foundation, CFR, EIA, Treasury, Goldman Sachs, Federal Reserve, OECD, Cato Institute, Penn Wharton Budget Model, IMF, USTR, ACLU.
